food_subgroup: Herbal tea / traditional Chinese medicinal beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to Wang Lao Ji / Wong Lo Kat exists; closest parent 'herbal tea' (FOODON_03301099) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KN (Herbal infusions) is the closest defensible parent; no specific Wang Lao Ji code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 'Herbal tea' used as closest parent; no product-specific code for Wang Lao Ji exists.

main_ingredients: herbal extracts (chrysanthemum, honeysuckle, licorice root, cogongrass rhizome, common selfheal spike)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard can of Wong Lo Kat herbal tea (approximately 310 ml / 310 g) as commonly sold in Singapore and convenience stores.

nutrient_source.title: Wang Lao Ji Herbal Tea (canned) — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; supplemented by product label data cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy values for sweetened herbal tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Wang Lao Ji branded product exists in FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for sweetened herbal tea beverages (NDB 14355 / similar entries) and cross-checked against the published product nutrition label (per 100 ml): energy ~45 kcal, carbohydrate ~11 g (of which sugars ~11 g), protein 0 g, fat 0 g, sodium ~10 mg. These values are consistent with the commercially available 310 ml can sold in Singapore.

gi_source.title: Fructose, sucrose and glucose solutions — GI values for sugar-sweetened beverages; analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened beverage GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Wang Lao Ji herbal tea. GI estimated at 52 based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sucrose-sweetened beverages (GI ~60–65 for pure sucrose solutions; adjusted downward to ~52 for dilute sugar-herbal tea matrix consistent with published GI values for lightly sweetened tea beverages). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 34.1) / 100 ≈ 17.7, where carbohydrate per 310 g serving = 11.0 × 3.1 = 34.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~10 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Wong Lo Kat herbal tea contains approximately 10 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it a low-sodium beverage that poses minimal direct risk for hypertension when consumed in moderate amounts; however, the added sugar content should be considered in the context of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar content (~11 g per 100 ml, ~34 g per can)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 310 ml can of Wong Lo Kat provides approximately 34 g of added sugar, contributing a glycemic load of ~17.7; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption and account for this carbohydrate load within their daily glucose management plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and cholesterol; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Wong Lo Kat herbal tea contains no fat or cholesterol, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, so intake should be moderated in individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wong Lo Kat (王老吉, also romanized as Wang Lao Ji or Wáng Lǎojí) is a traditional Cantonese herbal tea originating from Guangdong province, China, with a history spanning over 180 years. It is one of the most iconic and widely consumed Chinese herbal beverages in the world, sold primarily as a sweetened canned or bottled drink. The beverage is brewed from a proprietary blend of Chinese medicinal herbs including chrysanthemum (菊花), honeysuckle (金银花), licorice root (甘草), cogongrass rhizome (白茅根), and common selfheal spike (夏枯草), combined with water and sugar. The flavour profile is mildly sweet with a distinctive slightly bitter, cooling herbal finish, reflecting its traditional role in Chinese medicine as a 'heat-clearing' (清热) drink believed to reduce internal heat and prevent heatstroke. In Singapore, Wong Lo Kat is widely available in Chinese restaurants, hawker centres, and convenience stores, typically served chilled in its signature red can. Nutritionally, a 310 ml can provides approximately 140 kcal, derived almost entirely from approximately 34 g of added sugar, with negligible protein and fat. The beverage has a low-to-moderate glycemic index (~52) but a meaningful glycemic load per serving (~17.7) due to its sugar content. Clinically, it is low in sodium and fat but high in added sugar, making it a moderate concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The herbal ingredients have been studied for antioxidant properties, though clinical evidence for therapeutic benefit in the canned commercial form is limited.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Wong Lo Kat?
Is Wong Lo Kat good for diabetics?
What herbs are in Wang Lao Ji herbal tea?
Is 王老吉 suitable for people with high blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of Wong Lo Kat?
Is Wang Lao Ji the same as Jia Duo Bao?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Wang Lao Ji / Wong Lo Kat exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a named product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA sweetened herbal tea beverage data and cross-referenced with the commercially published product nutrition label. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sucrose-sweetened beverages. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of an exact approved-source match.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero — Wang Lao Ji herbal tea contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per product label and consistent with USDA sweetened herbal tea beverage data. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero — the product contains no fat by formulation; confirmed zero per product label. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for the commercial canned product. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported on the standard product label and not available in the adapted USDA source entry used. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 8. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 11. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 19. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 20. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 21. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 22.
